title: Privacy-safe profiles, mention rendering, and correct server-owner management controls (frontend)

requirements.summary: Remove all public display of other users’ Principal IDs across the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: When viewing any other user, no UI surface renders their Principal ID.
No lists (feed, servers, channels, DMs, search/autocomplete results, popovers) show Principal IDs.

file_operations.description: Remove any rendering of userPrincipal.toString() (Principal ID) for non-owner and owner contexts; ensure the popover only shows meaningful public profile fields (e.g., display name, avatar, bio/join date when available) and never exposes Principal IDs.
Remove the always-visible Principal ID text from the profile header area so the page does not reveal the Principal ID unless explicitly toggled by the signed-in user (REQ-19).

Remove any server list UI that could display a Principal ID (e.g., the current "by {server.owner}" text if it contains principal strings) and replace it with safe, meaningful owner display info derived from public profile data.

requirements.summary: Add a signed-in-user-only reveal/hide + copy control for the user’s own Principal ID in Profile & Settings (hidden by default).
acceptanceCriteria: On initial load of Profile/Settings, the Principal ID is not visible.
Clicking “Show my ID” reveals the Principal ID; clicking “Hide my ID” hides it again.
A “Copy” action copies the Principal ID to the clipboard and provides a visible success confirmation.

file_operations.description: Add a user-only section in Profile & Settings with stateful reveal/hide controls (hidden by default) for the signed-in user’s Principal ID and a copy-to-clipboard action using the existing toast system for success confirmation; do not render the Principal ID anywhere on this page unless the user explicitly reveals it.

requirements.summary: Update Profile & Settings UI to edit bio and manage per-field public/private visibility toggles for all supported profile fields (English text).
acceptanceCriteria: Profile/Settings includes an editable Bio field.
Profile/Settings includes a visibility toggle per supported field (e.g., Display name, Avatar, Bio, Join date, etc.).
Toggling visibility changes what other users can see on public profile surfaces.
All labels, helper text, button text, and confirmations are in English.

file_operations.description: Update profile-related hooks to use the backend’s current profile types (caller profile as ExtendedUserProfile, other-user profile as PublicUserProfile) and add a mutation hook to update per-field visibility via the backend capability that updates profile field visibility; ensure relevant queries are invalidated/refetched after visibility changes.

Refactor the profile form to align with supported fields (Display name, Avatar, Bio, Join date) and add a public/private visibility toggle per supported field using existing UI components; wire toggles to the visibility update hook and keep all user-facing text in English.

requirements.summary: Update all profile display surfaces to show meaningful public profile info (and only public fields) instead of Principal IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Public-facing profile surfaces display public fields (e.g., display name and avatar) when available.
Bio and join date only render for other users if those fields are marked public by the profile owner.
No public profile surface renders a Principal ID.

file_operations.description: Update the popover to render only public profile fields returned by public profile queries (e.g., displayName, avatar, bio, joinDate when present) and to never show Principal IDs; format and label fields in English and ensure missing fields do not render placeholders that imply private data.
Ensure post author UI uses public profile information (display name, avatar) and opens the updated ProfilePopover without exposing Principal IDs; confirm no fallback path renders Principal strings.

Ensure message author UI uses public profile information (display name, avatar) and opens the updated ProfilePopover without exposing Principal IDs; confirm no fallback path renders Principal strings.

requirements.summary: Fix server management controls visibility using server owner Principal returned by the backend for permission checks.
acceptanceCriteria: If the signed-in user is the server owner, server management actions are visible and usable.
If the signed-in user is not the server owner, server management actions are not shown (or are disabled) as appropriate.
Ownership/permission logic is based on Principal identity, not display name.

file_operations.description: Change the owner check to compare the signed-in user’s Principal string to server.ownerPrincipal (not server.owner), and keep edit/delete actions visible only for the true owner based on Principal identity.

Fix channel management visibility by comparing the signed-in user’s Principal to the selected server’s ownerPrincipal (not server owner display text), so create/edit/delete controls appear only for the actual server owner.
Update props passed into ServerList/ChannelList so the permission checks consistently use the signed-in user’s Principal and the server’s ownerPrincipal returned by the backend.

requirements.summary: Enhance @mention UX by formatting mentions in rendered text and making them clickable to open a user profile surface without exposing Principal IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Rendered message/post text visually distinguishes @mentions from surrounding text.
Clicking an @mention opens a profile surface for the mentioned user.
The UI does not reveal Principal IDs as part of mention rendering or interaction.

file_operations.description: Create a small resolver hook to map an @mention display name to a user Principal using existing user search capabilities, with lightweight caching so clicks can open a profile surface without showing Principal IDs.

Create a renderer component that parses message/post text, visually styles @mentions, and makes them clickable; on click, resolve the mention to a Principal and open a profile popover surface without exposing any Principal IDs in the UI.
Replace plain post content rendering with the MentionText component so @mentions are formatted and clickable while keeping the UI free of Principal IDs.
Replace plain message content rendering with the MentionText component so @mentions are formatted and clickable while keeping the UI free of Principal IDs.
